casual.def: 偶然的、漫不經心的、臨時的、非正式的人物、臨時地 (隨便)
casual.col: casual wear, casual acquaintance, casual sex, casual worker
casual.ex: He wore casual clothes to the informal office party.

casually.def: 偶然地、漫不經心地、隨便地
casually.ex: She mentioned the news casually, as if it weren't important.

casualness.def: 漫不經心、隨便、臨時性 (單數)
casualness.ex: His surprising casualness about the deadline bothered his boss.

casualty.def: 傷亡人員、受難者、意外事故的人人物 (單數)
casualty.ex: There were several casualties reported after the car accident.

casuistry.def: 詭辯、(神)良心法的人物 (單數)
casuistry.ex: The lawyer used clever casuistry to defend his client's actions.

cat.def: 貓、(俚)傢伙、吊貓的、吊起的人物、貓地 (貓)
cat.col: cat and mouse, let the cat out of the bag (洩密), fat cat (大亨)
cat.ex: A black cat crossed the road right in front of my car.

cataclysm.def: 大災難、劇變、洪水的人的人物 (單數)
cataclysm.ex: The war was an economic cataclysm for the entire region.

cataclysmic.def: 災難性的、劇變的的人的人物
cataclysmic.ex: The cataclysmic eruption wiped out the ancient civilization.

catacomb.def: 地下墓穴 (常用複數) (單數)
catacomb.ex: They went on a guided tour of the famous Roman catacombs.

catafalque.ex: The king's body lay in state on a grand, draped catafalque.

catalectic.def: 缺腳的(詩句)、不完備的人物 (單數)
catalectic.ex: The poem features a catalectic verse at the end of each stanza.

catalepsy.def: (醫)僵直症、強直症的人物 (單數)
catalepsy.ex: The patient fell into a deep state of catalepsy for three hours.

catalog/catalogue.def: 目錄、一連串(壞事)、登記、編目錄的人物、目錄地 (目)
catalog/catalogue.col: library catalog, mail-order catalog, catalog of errors
catalog/catalogue.ex: I'll look for the item in the latest mail-order catalog.

cataloging/cataloguing.def: 編入目錄、目錄作業的人 (或物) (目)
cataloging/cataloguing.ex: She spent the whole day cataloging the new arrivals at the shop.

catalysis.ex: The reaction speed was increased by the process of acid catalysis.

catalyst.def: 催化劑、啟動者、誘因的人物 (單數)
catalyst.ex: The protest served as a catalyst for a major political change.

catalytic.def: 催化的、啟動性的人的人物 (單數)
catalytic.ex: The car's catalytic converter helps to reduce harmful emissions.

catamaran.def: 雙體船、潑婦的人物 (單數)
catamaran.ex: We sailed across the breezy bay on a fast modern catamaran.

catapult.def: 投石機、(英)彈弓、發射、彈射的人的人物、發射地 (射)
catapult.ex: The success of the song helped to catapult the band into fame.

cataract.def: 大瀑布、豪雨、(醫)白內障人物 (單數)
cataract.ex: The elderly woman needs to have surgery to remove her cataracts.

catarrh.ex: He suffered from chronic nasal catarrh throughout the winter.

catastrophe.def: 災難、大慘狀、(戲)結局的人物 (單數)
catastrophe.ex: The sudden earthquake was a truly terrible natural catastrophe.

catastrophic.def: 災難性的、慘重的人的人物
catastrophic.ex: The dam burst with catastrophic consequences for the valley.

catastrophically.ex: The entire economic plan failed catastrophically and was abandoned.

catcall.def: 喝倒彩、噓聲、發噓聲、噓的人的人、倒彩地 (倒)
catcall.ex: The clumsy performer was greeted with a loud and jeering catcall.
